name: La Garganta de la Cabra — circular por la sierra de Almansa
region_intro: Mountain biking around Almansa offers diverse landscapes and varied terrain, situated on a high plateau at approximately 700 meters of altitude. The region features natural passages between mountain systems, contributing to its varied topography. Key physical features include the Sierra de Almansa, Sierra del Mugrón, and Monte Arabí, alongside the Almansa Reservoir. This varied environment provides a range of mountain bike trails suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Almansa The most popular mountain bike trail is Sierra del Mugrón and Almansa Reservoir Circular, a 30.6 miles (49.3 km) trail that takes 4 hours 5 minutes to complete. This route traverses areas with historical trenches and passes by the Almansa Reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Almansa and Santa Bárbara - Sierra de la Oliva Loop, a moderate 31.9 miles (51.4 km) path. This route offers diverse landscapes and views, passing by the Santa Bárbara religious building on the Sierra de la Oliva. Local mountain bikers also love the La Garganta de la Cabra — circular por la sierra de Almansa, a 34.1 miles (54.8 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Almansa, often completed in about 4 hours 44 minutes. Mountain biking around Almansa is defined by mountain ranges like the Sierra de Almansa, the Almansa Reservoir, and the varied terrain of Monte Arabí. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy to difficult. The routes in Almansa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 500 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Almansa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Almansa?
A: Almansa offers a wide selection of mountain bike trails, with over 90 routes available. These trails cater to various skill levels, from easy to difficult, ensuring there's something for every rider.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Almansa?
A: The terrain around Almansa is quite varied, characterized by its position on a high plateau at about 700 meters altitude, with natural passages between mountain systems. You'll encounter diverse landscapes ranging from the challenging slopes of the Sierra de Almansa and Sierra del Mugrón to the scenic surroundings of the Almansa Reservoir and the varied topography of Monte Arabí. The region's Mediterranean influences also contribute to its unique environment.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners or families in Almansa?
A: Yes, Almansa has trails suitable for beginners and families. Out of the over 90 routes, 14 are classified as easy, offering a gentler introduction to mountain biking in the region. These routes are generally less demanding in terms of elevation and technical skill.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in Almansa?
A: Almansa's climate, with its Mediterranean influences, generally offers good conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn are often ideal, providing pleasant temperatures. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are recommended, while winters are typically mild, allowing for year-round exploration.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Almansa?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike trails in Almansa are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1040861306/la-garganta-de-la-cabra-circular-por-la-sierra-de-almansa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Garganta de la Cabra — circular por la sierra de Almansa&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 54.8 km loop through the Sierra de Almansa. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036342633/sierra-del-mugron-and-almansa-reservoir-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra del Mugrón and Almansa Reservoir Circular&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 49.3 km route.

Q: What interesting landmarks or viewpoints can I find along the mountain bike trails?
A: Many trails offer access to significant landmarks and scenic viewpoints. You can pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5538804" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Bárbara&lt;/a&gt; religious building on the Sierra de la Oliva, or enjoy views from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4713383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Museum Trail&lt;/a&gt;. The iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5287878" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Almansa&lt;/a&gt; also provides excellent panoramic views of the surrounding area, often visible from nearby routes.

Q: Are there any trails that pass by the Almansa Reservoir?
A: Yes, the Almansa Reservoir is a prominent feature in the region and is integrated into several mountain biking routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036342633/sierra-del-mugron-and-almansa-reservoir-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra del Mugrón and Almansa Reservoir Circular&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, offering picturesque surroundings as you ride along its shores.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about Almansa?
A: The mountain bike trails in Almansa are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 250 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, the varied terrain that caters to different skill levels, and the scenic views, particularly around the Sierra de Almansa and the Almansa Reservoir.

Q: Are there any advanced or challenging mountain bike trails in Almansa?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Almansa offers 6 difficult trails. These routes often feature significant elevation gains and more technical sections, requiring good fitness and advanced riding skills. The Sierra de Almansa and Sierra del Mugrón areas are known for their more demanding terrain.

Q: Can I find amenities like cafes or pubs near the mountain bike trails in Almansa?
A: While specific cafes directly on remote trails might be limited, Almansa town itself, and some of the smaller villages you might pass through, offer various amenities including cafes, restaurants, and pubs where you can refuel and relax after your ride. Planning your route to pass through these points can enhance your experience.

Q: What is the typical duration for mountain bike trails in Almansa?
A: The duration of trails varies significantly based on their length and difficulty. For instance, a moderate route like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036350524/almansa-and-santa-barbara-sierra-de-la-oliva-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almansa and Santa Bárbara - Sierra de la Oliva Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 51.4 km, typically takes around 4 hours and 41 minutes to complete. Shorter, easier trails will take less time, while longer, more challenging routes can extend to 5 hours or more.

Q: Are there any trails that offer historical insights or pass by historical sites?
A: Yes, some routes integrate historical elements. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1036342633/sierra-del-mugron-and-almansa-reservoir-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra del Mugrón and Almansa Reservoir Circular&lt;/a&gt; traverses areas with historical trenches. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38267598" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casa Alfonso – Palace of the Counts of Cirat loop from Almansa&lt;/a&gt; passes by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5674298" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palace of the Counts of Cirat&lt;/a&gt;, offering a blend of outdoor activity and cultural exploration.
